feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: BAPI /SCWM/MFG_RECEIVE_HUS_EXT to Receive HUs for EWM MES integration
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP has provided standard function module /SCWM/MFG_RECEIVE_HUS_EXT to Receive HUs for EWM MES integration. Also EGR is mandatory for this BAPI.

This BAPI was working fine while using decentralized EWM as we create EGR . But in embedded EWM this BAPI was not helpful to receive HU's from production. Any suggestion on how to receive HU's from MES in embedded EWM without EGR .

Help is highly appreciated

entry.title: Stock room management vs Basic EWM vs EWM
entry.content:
I wonder if someone can direct me in these questions:

1. Is Stock Room Management a solution that is being phased out right now? End of life? This is "old wm?"

2. Is it still a valid options for implementations for mid-size companies? Key features that is missing in this?

3. Basic EWM... key features that is missing vs Full-EWM?

entry.title: SAP EWM Good s receipt "partially complete 1 of the HU IIIOUSA0011055 cannot be reversed
entry.content:
I have one Inb delivery ERP 70255415 , unloaded but partially put away . Good s receipt "partially complete as 1 of the HU IIIOUSA0011055 cannot be reversed, delete or put away , created by mistake. How can I complete the receiving for the remaining line item .It was It ticket  created from Business side.

Thanks,

Janmeet

entry.title: EWM Packspec create with 0 Qty
entry.content:
Hi Experts

Is there a way to make below value 0 in packspec, user will decide qty at the time of receipt.

entry.title: How is maximum length at /SAPAPO/MAT1 really working?
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I want to ask for your support with a question about the maximum length found in the material master data on EWM S/4HANA (/SAPAPO/MAT1, or MM03).

According to the documentation, this field specifies the maximum length of the products that can be packed inside the packaging material, and it differs from the length of a unit of the packaging material.

For testing purposes, I have maintained 4 CM as maximum length in the packaging material CTN_02 and I have packed in /SCWM/PACK a product with length 706 MM. I was expecting an error message, or at least a warning, but the system allowed me to go ahead with no restriction at all.

Once packed, the handling unit now had 24 CM of length, which is the length in the master data for the packaging material. But the product, which has 70.6 CM as length, was packed successfully into a packaging material with 4 CM as max. length.

So how does this maximum length actually works? Is is just an statistical value, or is it really restricting any products with longer lengths to be packed? If this should show an error/warning message, can you share a case where it actually works?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: ECC-EWM-GTS Export Declaration “Before PGI in EWM” -Bonded Warehouse
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Good Day

I have below requirement, kindly share your valuable inputs to define the FCD process in BWH.

Standard behaviour : SO -> Del -> ODO- > PGI in EWM ->OD->ECC-Material document-> GTS Export declaration, in this scenario export declaration created in GTS has all the required data and there is no issue here.

I think when the PGI is done in EWM, material document created in ECC, hope that is the reference to GTS for doing the “Export declaration”

Required behaviour: SO -> Del - > ODO->Picking Complete-> “NO” PGI in EWM -> Export declaration in GTS

This is for Free Circulation stocks.

Is there any process, with which we can do the “Export declaration” before PGI in EWM?

This is due to operational challenges as after PGI immediately the shipment leaves the facility and with in which if the customs clearance (from duty unpaid to duty paid) is not received, it will be an issue.

And hence we want to keep the PGI as last step, ie. After export declaration process is completed, business wants to print some carrier labels and want to post the goods issue and then shipment leaves physically. This would give enough time for FCD  process to customs.

Note: Client system is on EWM Decentral 9.5 and ECC 6.0

Look forward for your valuable advise.

Thanks,

Hari.

 

@sapewm SAP Community @GTS

entry.title: Mass upload of Customizing in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hii Everyone,

I have to do a customizing activity of maintaining Hu types for each storage bin type in SPRO>SCM>EWM>GOOD RECEIPT PROCESS>STRATEGIES>STORAGE BIN DETERMINATION>HU TYPES>DEFINE HU TYPES FOR EACH STORAGE BIN TYPES.

I have around 7000 entries that need to be maintained which is a lot to do manually. I was wondering if there was a way around it to do it by mass upload.

Any help and info is appreciated.

 

Best regards,

Rashmi Bhandari

entry.title: Addressing Inventory Mismatch in Warehousing using SAP S/4HANA EWM: A Case Study from Germany
entry.content:
Inventory mismatch is one of the most persistent and impactful challenges faced by businesses across industries, ranging from e-commerce to manufacturing. It affects not only sales order fulfillment but also leads to significant financial losses when discrepancies in inventory valuation arise. From my past projects, I recall one such case involving a manufacturing giant in Germany ,which highlights the importance of effective warehouse management systems and ERP integration.

The Problem: Missing Stock During Transportation. The client, a leading manufacturer, operates multiple small warehouses alongside a central production plant. Despite using SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) to manage stock at the warehouse level, discrepancies frequently arose. Stock often went missing during the short transport journey between the production plant and the warehouse.

A site analysis revealed the root of the issue:

1. Absence of ERP at the Production Plant:

The production plant lacked an appropriate ERP system like SAP PP (Production Planning) to manage stock effectively. Instead, it relied on outdated, paper-based counting systems, which led to errors, delays, and a lack of transparency.

2. Manual Processes at the Warehouse Level:

Even at the warehouse level, manual practices such as paper-based inventory counting and bin sorting were prevalent. These processes not only contributed to inventory mismatches but also significantly hampered operational efficiency.

The Impact of Inventory Mismatch: The financial repercussions for the company were considerable. Missing stock meant not only a direct loss in inventory value but also disruptions in production and delayed order fulfillment. These inefficiencies cascaded across the supply chain, impacting customer satisfaction and the company’s bottom line.

The Solution: Implementing Robust SAP EWM Features.

To address these challenges, we implemented a series of measures leveraging SAP EWM’s capabilities:

1. SAP EWM Deconsolidation:

Deconsolidation is the process of breaking down large incoming shipments into smaller, more manageable units for efficient put-away and retrieval. By implementing SAP EWM's deconsolidation functionality, we ensured that products were properly sorted and organized before being stored in the warehouse. This streamlined the process and reduced errors during inventory handling.

2. SAP EWM Physical Inventory Counting:

We introduced SAP EWM’s physical inventory counting features to replace manual counting methods. This enabled automated and accurate inventory checks, reducing discrepancies and enhancing overall transparency.

3. SAP PP-EWM Integration at the Production Plant Level:

To address the lack of ERP at the production plant, we integrated SAP PP with SAP EWM. This integration provided real-time visibility into inventory movement from the production plant to the warehouse. It also automated key processes, ensuring seamless tracking and synchronization of stock levels.

Results Achieved:

The implementation of these solutions delivered several tangible benefits to the client:

Improved Transparency:

Real-time data flow between the production plant and the warehouse eliminated blind spots in inventory tracking.

Reduced Financial Losses:

By minimizing inventory mismatches, the company significantly reduced the financial impact of missing stock.

Enhanced Efficiency:

Automation replaced manual processes, speeding up operations and allowing employees to focus on higher-value tasks.

Better Order Fulfillment:

With accurate inventory data, the company could fulfill sales orders more reliably, improving customer satisfaction.

Key Takeaways:

This case underscores the critical importance of integrating robust ERP and warehouse management systems in addressing inventory mismatches. Here are some key lessons learned:

1. ERP Integration is Essential:

A lack of ERP tools at the production plant was the primary cause of the issue. Integrating SAP PP with SAP EWM provided the necessary transparency and control.

2. Eliminate Manual Processes:

Paper-based systems are prone to errors and inefficiencies. Transitioning to automated processes not only reduces discrepancies but also enhances operational efficiency.

3. Leverage Advanced Features of SAP EWM:

Functions like deconsolidation and physical inventory counting are powerful tools for optimizing warehouse operations. Companies should utilize these features to address specific challenges.

4. Adopt a Holistic Approach:

Warehouse management doesn’t exist in isolation. Issues at the production plant level can ripple through the supply chain. A holistic approach, addressing both production and warehousing, is crucial.

Conclusion: 

Inventory mismatch is a common yet solvable issue that affects businesses across industries. By adopting the right technologies and integrating processes, companies can mitigate losses, improve efficiency, and enhance customer satisfaction. The German manufacturing giant’s case serves as a testament to the transformative impact of leveraging SAP EWM and ERP tools effectively. For organizations looking to streamline their operations, this approach provides a blueprint for success.#SAPEWM SAP Supply Chain Management  SAP Extended Warehouse Management SAP ERP, add-on for embedded production planning and detailed scheduling

entry.title: Serialized product stock upload into WM using migration template
entry.content:
While uploading stock for serialized material into warehouse bin, it is throwing error: Serial numbers not allowed in non-serialized product (000001) .

But the product is serialized and already serial numbered stock is existing.

entry.title: Confirm Warehouse Task call failing from a custom SAP FIORI app to S/4HANA Public Cloud API
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to call action ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact for a existing open Warehouse task in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud via following code :

const sPathAct = `/WarehouseTask(EWMWarehouse='Z10',WarehouseTask='1000000232',WarehouseTaskItem='0')/com.sap.gateway.srvd_a2x.api_warehouse_order_task_2.v0001.ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact(...)`
const oAction = oModel.bindContext(sPathAct
     ,undefined  //null
     ,{
         "DirectWhseTaskConfIsAllowed": true

      }

                            ); 

     // Execute the action
oAction.invoke(undefined,true).then( function(oResult) {
   MessageToast.show("Warehouse task confirmed successfully.");
   }).catch(function(oError) {
      MessageToast.show(oError.toString());
   });    

and I am facing following error:

Error: Not a bound action: /WarehouseTask(EWMWarehouse='Z10',WarehouseTask='1000000232',WarehouseTaskItem='0')/com.sap.gateway.srvd_a2x.api_warehouse_order_task_2.v0001.ConfirmWarehouseTaskExact(...)

    at l.createCacheAndRequest (ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:650:10)

    at ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:256:17

    at SyncPromise-dbg.js:314:14

    at e (SyncPromise-dbg.js:63:4)

    at new r (SyncPromise-dbg.js:230:3)

    at r.then (SyncPromise-dbg.js:313:7)

    at l._invoke (ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:236:5)

    at l.invoke (ODataContextBinding-dbg.js:1413:15)

    at c.onMovePallet (MovePalletsNewBinToPallet.controller.js:203:33)               


I double checked and  the following entity does exist in the S4H backend system:
/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask(EWMWarehouse='Z10',WarehouseTask='1000000232',WarehouseTaskItem='0')

What could be wrong with my code ?

Thanks!

Br
Mikko

entry.title: PPF message not always processed, stucked in SPPFP (SMQS)
entry.content:
Hi guys,

we are a few days before the start of operations at the customer plant and we are encountering the problem that PPF actions are not always processed correctly. When the GI is posted in EWM, a PPF message is created, sometimes it passes it well (message processed and GI is posted in ERP), sometimes it gets stuck on SPPFP as a yellow mark and by manual execution it makes a statement as Time timit exceeded

In trsc SMQS it looks like this:

Target NONE should not be empty, but it is not:

We are encountering this problem on the quality system, I assume it came from many actions being run in a short period of time, but how is it possible that some of them are works and some are not? I also changed the "Max. Conn" value in SMQS for the target time from 10 to 12, but it had no effect...

I am are grateful for your advices

entry.title: EWM SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, Warehouse Attributes
entry.content:
Hi, 

does anybody know if there is any way to change the Warehouse Attributes - Unit of Length in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition after it has been defined once? Can it be somehow adjusted if there are not so many movements in the system until now?

The standard UoM - m has been configured and I'm wondering if it still can be changed to cm.

Besides, do you know for the time unit in the warehouse attributes is needed?   

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: I am getting the below error while conforming process order for non ewm managed materials using WM
entry.content:
Could not determine EWM stock data - mapping storage location

Hi, we are using WM in S4hana(EWM not active). but still we are getting the above issue. 

 

Any idea what could be reason? I have checked all the configuration but did not find any config that is related to EWM for the plant and storage location

 

any help is highly appreciated

Thanks

Ranveer

entry.title: Planned Delivery Date does not update from WM to ERP - Inbound Delivery - S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
If you change a planned delivery date on a PO - Inbound delivery via Change Inbound Deliveries - Deliveries. It does not update the ERP side's Planned Delivery Date. I assume this is not intended as this will have further impacts on MRP such as Manage Material Coverage app. What would be the standard solution for this? Delete the delivery and create new?

We are using Ariba so deleting an inbound delivery and creating new would not be ideal.

 

See Delivery from WM side, via Change Inbound Deliveries - Deliveries. Planned Delivery Date has been changed.

See Delivery Date from ERP side via VL32N (Not updated).

 

Hi all,

After further investigation, it seems that changing the delivery date does not replicate back to ERP side if the delivery date is changed alone. If we change the carrier, it will replicate back. Furthermore, by changing the delivery date and posting GR, it does NOT change the delivery date either. We are currently testing to see if a custom field will also enable this change of the delivery date.

entry.title: \/SCWM/PWR801,802 when Prod Order released to create PMR in EWM
entry.content:
hello Team, 

Our Production order when released , queue is getting stuck with the error "Quantity role Staging for Production Planned (W6) required for single-order staging " .  This is new upgrade box S/4 HANA 2023 . 

we did not see this error before.

entry.title: Automatic packaging using palletization 0PAL in inbound process
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Can we use 0PAL packaging process for automatic HU creation like 0IBD? secondly when I use 0PAL it is asking for destination HUs so how to generate those HUs ?

Please help.

entry.title: Unable to open the application log from the warehouse monitor message queue
entry.content:
Hello EWM experts,

 After creating the SLG1(for MFS process)  logs using FM '/SCWM/MFS_LOG_ADD_FM ' and '/SCWM/MFS_LOG_END_ADD' and '/SCWM/MFS_WRITE_LOG' we are unable to display the same log from the application log button from the /SCWM/MON->message queue node for the error. Also one observation is that, it is not having any message ID and message number. 

Do we need to write any specific logic to display the same from the message queue? Any example logic?

Regards,

MR

entry.title: Question for EWM (on S4HANA) SAP template security roles in Obsolete status
entry.content:
We are going to create the new EWM system customizing security roles by referring to the system template roles /SCWM/ following the master guide, but noticed all /SCWM/ roles are in Obsolete status while not inactive status. Should we still be able to use these "Obsolete" template roles to create our new customizing roles?

RoleShort Description 
/SCWM/ANALYSTEWM: Warehouse AnalystObsolete 
/SCWM/BCV_USEREWM: Business Context Viewer UserObsolete 
/SCWM/COUNTEREWM: Physical Inventory CounterObsolete 
/SCWM/DAS_ADMINISTRATORAdministrator for Dock Appointment SchedulingObsolete 
/SCWM/DAS_CP_CLERKCheckpoint Clerk for Dock Appointment SchedulingObsolete 
/SCWM/DAS_EXT_CARR_PLANNERAppointment Planner for Carrier in Dock Appointment SchedulingObsolete 
/SCWM/DAS_SPECIALISTSpecialist for Dock Appointment SchedulingObsolete 
/SCWM/DAS_TO_EWM_INTEGRATIONTechnical Role for Dock Appointment Scheduling Integration to EWMObsolete 
/SCWM/ERP_EWM_INTEGRATIONEWM: ERP IntegrationObsolete 
/SCWM/EWM_TO_DAS_INTEGRATIONTechnical Role for EWM Integration to Dock Appointment SchedulingObsolete 
/SCWM/EXPERTEWM: Warehouse ExpertObsolete 
/SCWM/EXPERT_2Obsolete after 7.02 – use role /SCWM/EXPERTObsolete 
/SCWM/IDM_EWM_INTEGRATIONEWM: Identity Management IntegrationObsolete 
/SCWM/INBD_SPECIALISTEWM: Warehouse Specialist for Goods ReceiptObsolete 
/SCWM/INFORMATIONEWM: Display of Warehouse InformationObsolete 
/SCWM/INVENTORY_PLANNEREWM: Inventory PlannerObsolete 
/SCWM/LM_PLANNEREWM: Labor PlannerObsolete 
/SCWM/LM_SPECIALISTEWM: Labor SpecialistObsolete 
/SCWM/MENU_S4Menu for Extended Warehouse Management in S/4HANA 
/SCWM/OUTBD_SPECIALISTEWM: Warehouse Specialist for Goods IssueObsolete 
/SCWM/SO_CLERKEWM: Shipping Office ClerkObsolete 
/SCWM/SUPERVISOREWM: Warehouse SupervisorObsolete 
/SCWM/WB_ADMINISTRATORAdministrator for Warehouse BillingObsolete 
/SCWM/WB_SPECIALISTSpecialist for Warehouse BillingObsolete 
/SCWM/WORKEREWM: Warehouse WorkerObsolete 
/SCWM/YARD_SPECIALISTEWM: Warehouse Specialist for Yard ManagementObsolete

entry.title: Select delivery documents by creation date
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to select deliveries based on the following criteria via class /scwm/cl_dlv_management_prd and method query:

  • DOCCAT: PDO
  • ITEMTYPE: ODTS
  • Creation Date: Given Range selected by the user 

My code looks like this:

Selection table:

No documents are found if selection criteria for creation date (CRETST_H) is included. If I remove this then a document is found (with CRETST in the given range):

What am I doing wrong?

 

Best regards,

Tim

entry.title: Stop creation inspection lot for STO in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

when Goods receipt is posted for the inbound delivery created from STO inspection lot is created. We don't want to create inspection lot for STO inbound delivery as the batch was already inspected and confirmed in the another plant.

I am working on S4 Hana embedded system 2023. I changed inspection lot summary with Batch/Product in material master but still its creating inspection lot.

Kindly Advise how to stope the IL creation.

Regards,

 

Chand Basha.S
